meta.title: PPE Matrix — Line-3 Fettling Cell
meta.subtitle: Task×hazard→PPE selection (controls-first gate), fettler + grinder roles
meta.author: HSE Manufacturing — PPE Program
meta.classification: Internal — special-category respiratory-clearance data — competent-person review required
meta.deid_notice: All workers are referred to by role label. Respiratory medical-clearance / fitness-for-respirator data is reported at role level with <5 small-cell suppression; identifiers were pseudonymised before drafting and the re-identification key is held separately, not part of this document.

sections.text: This PPE matrix covers the named line-3 fettling cell (fettler and grinder roles) at Plant 3: manual grinding and deburring of cast components. The matrix is task-specific — not a site-wide PPE sheet. Each PPE row is specified ONLY for the residual hazard that survives the higher-order controls; a hazard with no higher-order control considered is recorded as a controls-first flag, not a PPE row.

rows.title: Eye/face — grinding-spark & fragment ejection
rows.description: Manual grinding ejects sparks and abrasive fragments toward the operator's face.

rows.title: Hearing — sustained grinding noise
rows.description: Continuous grinding produces sustained noise across the shift in the cell.

rows.title: Hand/arm — abrasion & vibration
rows.description: Hand-held grinder contact and workpiece edges present abrasion and hand-arm vibration.

rows.title: Respiratory — metal/silica dust
rows.description: Dry fettling of cast components generates airborne metal and silica dust.
rows.evidence: Task observation; dust monitoring [GAP — sample pending]

sections.text: For each body-region hazard, the higher-order controls (eliminate / substitute / engineer / administrative) are confirmed applied or justified BEFORE any PPE is specified. PPE is the residual-only last line. A hazard with no higher-order control recorded is a controls-first FLAG — the PPE cell is withheld, not invented.

rows: Fixed grinding screen + tool-mounted guard (engineering); job rotation limits exposure (administrative)
Damped grinding bench + enclosure panels (engineering); rotation (administrative)
Local exhaust ventilation (LEV) at the grinding bench (engineering); wet-fettling substitution under trial (substitution)
No engineering or administrative control recorded for this hazard
CONTROLS-FIRST FLAG — higher-order controls not considered; assess and apply (anti-vibration mounts, low-vibration tools, exposure limits) before specifying PPE. PPE row WITHHELD.
sections.caption: Controls-first gate per body-region hazard — PPE follows only the residual hazard

sections.text: Hand/arm hazard is a controls-first flag: no higher-order control was recorded. No PPE row is issued for it until the higher-order controls are assessed and applied or justified — PPE-first is not permitted.
PPE selection matrix (residual hazard → PPE type + cited standard)

rows: FFP3 / N95-class respirator (fit-test + clearance required, role level)
— not specified until higher-order controls applied —
sections.caption: PPE specified only for the residual hazard surviving the higher-order controls; each protection level cited to its conformity standard

items: Respiratory PPE (FFP3/N95) requires fit-testing and medical clearance — recorded at ROLE level only; no named worker's clearance result is carried into this document (<5 cells suppressed).
Face shield + safety glasses + ear defenders + respirator are checked for mutual compatibility (no seal break on the respirator from eyewear arms).
Training on correct donning/doffing, inspection, and replacement frequency is administrative support to PPE — NOT a substitute for the higher-order controls.

rows: Plant 3 — line-3 fettling cell (fettler + grinder roles)
Eye/face, hearing, respiratory, hand/arm (per OSHA 1910 Subpart I)
Site HSE Manager (named role; no individual identifier)
sections.caption: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.132(d)(2) written certification that the PPE hazard assessment was performed — mandatory, never omitted

sections.text: Hierarchy of controls (PPE as residual-only last line)

rows.control: Trial wet-fettling to substitute the dry dust-generating process
Maintain LEV capture + grinding screens; assess anti-vibration controls for the hand/arm hazard
Job rotation to limit grinding-noise and vibration exposure
Residual-only PPE per the matrix, against the cited conformity standard

rows.action: Assess and apply higher-order controls for the hand/arm hazard (anti-vibration mounts, low-vibration tools, exposure limits) before specifying PPE — clears the controls-first flag
Complete respirator fit-testing + medical clearance for the fettler role (recorded at role level)
Collect the pending dust sample to confirm the respiratory residual ([GAP] closed)

items: Review trigger: on task change / on PPE change / annual — whichever is first.
This PPE matrix is decision-support; a competent person must review it before use.
Regulatory basis: OSHA 29 CFR 1910 Subpart I (1910.132 + .133/.135/.136/.138); ISO 45001 §6.1.2 / §8.1.2; PPE conformity standards (EN / ANSI) cited per row.
